title: A method and a system for continuous monitoring of safety and basic life functions of patients
abstract: The method for continuous monitoring of safety and basic vital functions of patients is characterized in that the designated monitored facility is within the radio beacon network (RD) coverage in such a way that in every part of the monitored rooms (PMM) diagnostic band (OD) and fall detector (DU) located on the hip belt, provided for each patient (PD), communicate with at least three beacons (RD), performing continuous monitoring via the central beacon (RC) and the IT system monitoring centre (ICMS), in the form of current information transmitted to medical staff (PM) equipped with bands (OP) informing on the whereabouts of patients (PD) in monitored rooms (PMM). In the event of an alarm signal triggered by a fall signal being sent by a fall detector (DU) and/or a signal sent by a diagnostic band (OD) concerning an exceeded threshold value of the measured blood pressure, pulse, patient's (PD) body temperature, or ambient humidity change signal, or concerning the event of leaving the facility by patients (PD), the signal will initiate the alarm procedure in the scope of providing assistance. The system as per the invention includes fixed beacons (RS) in monitored rooms (PMM) located in fixed mapped locations, where patients (PD) equipped with diagnostic bands (OD) and fall detectors (DU) reside along with medical personnel (PM) and/or doctors (L) equipped with personnel bands (OP). In a separate room there is a central beacon (RC) connected by a communication line with the IT system monitoring centre (ICMS). The diagnostic bands (OD), fall detectors (DU), staff bands (OP) and fixed beacons (RS) with interfaces (CAN) via radio modules (MR) installed in them are connected with each other by means of a bidirectional wireless communication (LBP).
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A method for continuous monitoring of the safety and basic vital functions of patients based on continuous remote monitoring of vital parameters of patients using wrist bands and wireless remote measurement data transmission, characterized in that the designated monitored object is within the range of the radio beacon network (RD) coverage in a way that in every part of the monitored rooms (PMM) the diagnostic band (OD) and fall detector (DU) located on the hip belt provided for each patient (PD) communicate with at least three beacons (RD), performing ongoing monitoring through the central beacon (RC) and the IT system monitoring centre (ICMS), in the form of providing current information to medical personnel (PM) equipped with bands (OP) providing information about the whereabouts of the patients (PD) in the monitored rooms (PMM), and in case of alarm being activated in the event of fall sent by a fall detector (DU) and/or a signal transmitted by a diagnostic band (OD) concerning exceeding the threshold values of measured blood pressure, pulse, patient's (PD) body temperature, or ambient humidity change signal, or concerning circumstance of leaving facility by the patients (PD), result the initiation of the alarm procedure in the scope of providing assistance. 2. A method for continuous monitoring as per claim 1 characterized in that the blood pressure measurement is initiated and determined at the point of application of the patient's (PD) finger of the hand without the diagnostic band (OD) and simultaneously to the proximity sensor (ZCZ), external electrode (EZ) and to the external wrist optical pulse wave measurement from the applied finger module (ZMFT) located on the diagnostic band (OD). 3. The system for continuous monitoring of safety and basic life functions of patients composed of a measuring band is made in the form similar to a wrist watch for monitoring selected human life parameters, where the watch body contains a multi-functional integrated circuit with a processor, a heart rate monitor to monitor the user's heartbeat, an electrocardiogram sensor to monitor electrocardiogram data of the user, and wireless remote measurement data transmission to the computer device, generating alarms for medical personnel characterized in that fixed beacons (RS) in monitored rooms (PMM) are placed in fixed mapped locations, where the patients (PD) equipped with diagnostic bands (OD) and fall detector (DU) reside along with medical personnel (PM) and/or doctors (L) equipped with personnel bands (OP) while in a separate room there is a central beacon (RC) connected by a communication line with the IT system monitoring centre (ICMS); with the provision that diagnostic bands (OD), fall detectors (DU), personnel bands (OP) and fixed beacons (RS) with 

 interfaces (CAN) via the radio modules (MR) installed in tnem, are connected with each other by bidirectional wireless communication (LBP). 4. System as per claim 3 characterized in that the diagnostic band (OD) of patient (PD) consists of a processor (P) connected to radio module (MR), display (W), inertia measurement module (MB), ambient temperature measurement module (MTO), humidity measurement module (MW), charging and communication connector (USB), internal capacitive proximity sensor (WCZ), skin temperature measurement module (MTS), wrist optical pulse measurement module (MT), control button (PS), buzzer (B), vibration motor (SW), external electrode (EZ), and internal electrode (EW) via the wrist optical heart rate measurement module (MEKG), external optical sensor (ZCO) for pulse wave measurement and external proximity sensor (ZCZ), while the charging and communication connector (USB) is connected in series with the battery charging system (UL), battery (AK), and with converter blocks (DC/DC). 5. System as per claim 3 or 4 characterized in that the diagnostic band (OD) holds an external electrode (EZ) with an external sensor of the wrist optical pulse measurement module (ZMFT) located therein and an external proximity sensor (ZCZ). 6. System as per claim 5 characterized in that in the lower plane of the casing (OW) of the diagnostic band (OD) there is an internal electrode (EW) with an internal optical sensor of the wrist pulse monitoring module (MT), an internal proximity sensor (WCZ) and a skin temperature measurement sensor (MTS), as well as a charging and communication connector (USB). 7. System as per claim 3 characterized in that the fall detector (DU) consists of a processor (Pu) connected to a radio module (MRu), an inertia measurement module (MB U ), a charging and communication connector (USBu), a control button (PS U ), a buzzer (B u ) anf a vibration motor (SW U ), while the charging and communication connector (USBu) is connected in series with the battery charging system (UL U ), the battery (AK U ), and with converter blocks (DC/DC U ). 8. System as per claim 7 characterized in that the inertia measurement module (MB U ) consists of a block for digital signal processing, calibration, compensation, and orientation change (BPSK) to which an accelerometer (ACC) is connected via an analogue-to-digital converter (ADCa), a gyroscope (ZYR), a fall detection block (BDU) and a step counting block (BZK) are connected via an analogue-to-digital converter (ADCz), while analogue-digital converters (ADC a ) and (ADCz) are connected to a quartz oscillator (OSC).
